     run_test "Doc covers mTLS" "grep -q 'mTLS' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/docs/third-party-integrations.md'"
 
     # Setup.sh integration
     run_test "setup.sh references provider profiles" "grep -q 'cc-provider' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
     run_test "setup.sh creates providers directory" "grep -q 'providers' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
 
     # CLAUDE.md integration
     run_test "CLAUDE.md documents cc-provider" "grep -q 'cc-provider' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/CLAUDE.md'"
 
     # gwt-ticket --provider integration
     run_test "gwt-ticket has --provider flag" "grep -q '\-\-provider' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/fish/functions/gwt-ticket.fish'"
     run_test "gwt-ticket --provider parses conf file" "grep -q 'provider_profile' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/fish/functions/gwt-ticket.fish'"
     run_test "gwt-ticket --provider in help text" "grep -q 'provider.*bedrock.*vertex.*foundry' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/fish/functions/gwt-ticket.fish'"
     run_test "gwt-ticket --provider in examples" "grep -q 'provider bedrock' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/fish/functions/gwt-ticket.fish'"
 
     rm -f "$tmp_conf" 2>/dev/null
 }
 
 test_gitattributes() {
     echo -e "${BLUE}--- Gitattributes Tests ---${NC}"
     run_test ".gitattributes exists" "[ -f '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.gitattributes' ]"
     run_test "JSON diff driver script exists" "[ -x '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/git-diff-json.sh' ]"
     run_test "Union merge driver script exists" "[ -x '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/merge-driver-union.sh' ]"
 
     # Verify key attributes are applied
     run_test "CLAUDE.md has union-doc merge" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr merge -- CLAUDE.md | grep -q 'union-doc'"
     run_test "Shell scripts enforce LF" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr eol -- scripts/setup.sh | grep -q 'lf'"
     run_test "Fish files enforce LF" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr eol -- .config/fish/config.fish | grep -q 'lf'"
     run_test "PNG files marked binary" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr binary -- generated-diagrams/diagram_335a360a.png | grep -q 'set'"
     run_test "JSON files use json diff driver" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr diff -- .config/vscode/settings.json | grep -q 'json'"
     run_test "Plist files use plist diff driver" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr diff -- Library/LaunchAgents/com.dotfiles.opencode-serve.plist | grep -q 'plist'"
 
     # Verify JSON diff driver produces valid output
     run_test "JSON diff driver produces sorted output" "bash '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/git-diff-json.sh' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/vscode/settings.json' | head -1 | grep -q '{'"
 
     # Verify merge drivers are assigned
     run_test "Brewfile uses brewfile merge driver" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr merge -- homebrew/Brewfile | grep -q 'brewfile'"
     run_test "settings.json uses json-merge driver" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr merge -- .claude/settings.json | grep -q 'json-merge'"
     run_test "config.fish uses union-doc merge" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr merge -- .config/fish/config.fish | grep -q 'union-doc'"
     run_test "test-filter.sh uses union-doc merge" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr merge -- scripts/test-filter.sh | grep -q 'union-doc'"
     run_test "lazy-lock.json uses lockfile merge" "git -C '$DOTFILES_ROOT' check-attr merge -- .config/nvim/lazy-lock.json | grep -q 'lockfile'"
 
     # Verify merge driver scripts exist and are executable
     run_test "Brewfile merge driver exists" "[ -x '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/merge-driver-brewfile.sh' ]"
     run_test "JSON merge driver exists" "[ -x '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/merge-driver-json.sh' ]"
     run_test "Lockfile merge driver exists" "[ -x '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/merge-driver-lockfile.sh' ]"
 
     # Verify setup.sh registers all drivers
     run_test "setup.sh registers JSON diff driver" "grep -q 'diff.json.textconv' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
     run_test "setup.sh registers plist diff driver" "grep -q 'diff.plist.textconv' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
     run_test "setup.sh registers union-doc merge driver" "grep -q 'merge.union-doc.driver' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
     run_test "setup.sh registers brewfile merge driver" "grep -q 'merge.brewfile.driver' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
     run_test "setup.sh registers json-merge driver" "grep -q 'merge.json-merge.driver' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
     run_test "setup.sh registers lockfile merge driver" "grep -q 'merge.lockfile.driver' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup.sh'"
 }
 
 print_summary() {
     echo ""
     echo -e "${BLUE}--- Summary ---${NC}"
     echo -e "  Total: ${TESTS_RUN}  ${GREEN}Pass: ${TESTS_PASSED}${NC}  ${RED}Fail: ${TESTS_FAILED}${NC}"
     if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
         exit 1
     fi
 }
 
 # Main
 GROUP="${1:-all}"
 
 case "$GROUP" in
 --list | -l)
     list_groups
     exit 0
     ;;
 fish) test_fish ;;
 stow) test_stow ;;
 claude) test_claude ;;
 gemini) test_gemini ;;
 pi) test_pi ;;
 setup-syntax) test_setup_syntax ;;
 brewfile) test_brewfile ;;
 mcp) test_mcp ;;
 browser) test_browser ;;
 tmux) test_tmux ;;
 hooks) test_hooks ;;
 agents-md) test_agents_md ;;
 subagents) test_subagents ;;
 cd-perf) test_cd_perf ;;
 lsp) test_lsp ;;
 nvim-bridge) test_nvim_bridge ;;
 remote-control) test_remote_control ;;
 settings) test_settings ;;
 entire) test_entire ;;
 gitattributes) test_gitattributes ;;
 merge-driver) bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/test-merge-driver.sh" ;;
 openclaw) bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/openclaw/test-openclaw.sh" ;;
 integrations) test_integrations ;;
 opencode) test_opencode ;;
 all)
     test_fish
     test_stow
     test_claude
     test_gemini
     test_pi
     test_setup_syntax
     test_brewfile
     test_mcp
     test_browser
     test_tmux
     test_hooks
     test_agents_md
     test_subagents
     test_cd_perf
     test_lsp
     test_nvim_bridge
     test_remote_control
     test_settings
     test_entire
     test_integrations
     test_opencode
     test_gitattributes
     # OpenClaw tests run from their own script (separate counters)
     # External test suites run as subprocesses (own set -e / counters)
     bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/test-merge-driver.sh"
     echo ""
     bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/openclaw/test-openclaw.sh"
     ;;
 *)
     echo "Unknown test group: $GROUP"
     echo ""
     list_groups
     exit 1
     ;;
 esac
 
 print_summary

parts.text: Implement this

**Adjusting configurations**

I’m looking into editing the opencode config next. I think I may need to test the JSON and the opencode filter. Also, the user had made a prior commit, and it seems like the current .gitignore might have some modifications that are uncommitted. It’s probably better if I don’t commit those changes. I’ll aim to use apply_patch instead. Let's make sure everything is correctly set up!

I’ll update the OpenCode permission config to preserve the current allow-by-default behavior while making repeated identical tool calls prompt via `doom_loop: "ask"`, then validate the JSON/config tests.

state.title: Run OpenCode filtered tests

parts.text:
**Investigating file failures**

I need to find a failure. Maybe it’s about config permissions being the issue? I should run a command to output the file or grep for failures. Possibly, I can use a Bash command like `rg` to look for "FAIL" in the output file. I'll consider using a `read` command for the file path. Should I employ the grep tool on the file since I know its output path? Let’s explore using Bash or `rg` for this.
The JSON is valid, but one OpenCode filtered test failed. I’m checking the specific failure and will update the existing test expectation if it still assumes the old string-only permission shape.

state.output:
Found 1 matches
/Users/shahe.islam/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_e849b1224001Y419zQfaT53KlG:
  Line 4: [0;31m  FAIL[0m OpenCode permissions are blanket allow
state.title: FAIL

state.output:
Found 2 matches
/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/test-filter.sh:
  Line 898:     run_test "settings has allow rules" "python3 -c \"import json; d=json.load(open('$SETTINGS')); assert len(d['permissions']['allow']) > 0\""

  Line 1062:     run_test "OpenCode permissions are blanket allow" "[ \"\$(jq -r '.permission' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json')\" = 'allow' ]"
state.title: OpenCode permissions are blanket allow|permission.*allow

parts.text: The failing test is specifically asserting the old blanket string value. I’m updating it to assert allow-by-default plus `doom_loop: ask`, which preserves the intended behavior.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/test-filter.sh
@@
-    run_test "OpenCode permissions are blanket allow" "[ \"\$(jq -r '.permission' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json')\" = 'allow' ]"
+    run_test "OpenCode permissions allow by default" "jq -e '.permission[\"*\"] == \"allow\" and .permission.doom_loop == \"ask\"' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json' >/dev/null 2>&1"
*** End Patch

state.output:
Success. Updated the following files:
M scripts/test-filter.sh

metadata.diff:
Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/test-filter.sh
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/test-filter.sh
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/test-filter.sh
@@ -1058,9 +1058,9 @@
 echo -e "${BLUE}--- OpenCode Tests ---${NC}"
 
 run_test "OpenCode config exists" "[ -f '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json' ]"
 run_test "OpenCode config is valid JSON" "jq empty '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json'"
-run_test "OpenCode permissions are blanket allow" "[ \"\$(jq -r '.permission' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json')\" = 'allow' ]"
+run_test "OpenCode permissions allow by default" "jq -e '.permission[\"*\"] == \"allow\" and .permission.doom_loop == \"ask\"' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json' >/dev/null 2>&1"
 run_test "OpenCode config has openai provider" "jq -e '.provider.openai' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json' >/dev/null 2>&1"
 run_test "OpenCode config has gpt-5.4 model" "jq -e '.provider.openai.models[\"gpt-5.4\"]' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/opencode/opencode.json' >/dev/null 2>&1"
 run_test "OpenCode Vim tap configured" "grep -q 'tap \"leohenon/tap\"' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/homebrew/Brewfile'"
 run_test "OpenCode Vim formula configured" "grep -q 'brew \"leohenon/tap/ocv\"' '$DOTFILES_ROOT/homebrew/Brewfile'"

parts.text:
Implemented `doom_loop: "ask"` while preserving allow-by-default permissions.

Changed:
- `.config/opencode/opencode.json`
  - Replaced `"permission": "allow"` with:
    ```json
    "permission": {
      "*": "allow",
      "doom_loop": "ask"
    }
    ```
- `scripts/test-filter.sh`
  - Updated the OpenCode permission test to validate the new object shape.

Validation:
- `jq empty .config/opencode/opencode.json` passed.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` passed: `185/185`.

Note: `git status` also shows `.tmux/resurrect/last` modified, which I did not change. OpenCode needs a restart to load the config change.
[Image 1] Why does this keep showing, it's constantly flickering which is annoying, what does this number mean?

**Investigating status display**

I'm in plan mode with a read-only setup and I need to get some answers. I think I should likely take a screenshot of the opencode status line since the timestamp looks repeated. It might help to inspect the configurations or status plugins, maybe even check the tmux status. I could use read/grep commands on this. The image shows a status line with a number that might be an opencode session ID, so I’ll search for more information.
I’ll trace which OpenCode/tmux status component renders that right-side numeric field, read-only, then explain what it represents and how to stop the flicker.

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: # Probe the OpenAI API to detect whether the current account is rate-limited.
5: # Reads the OAuth access token from OpenCode's auth.json and makes a minimal
6: # chat completion request (gpt-4o-mini, max_tokens=1).
7: #
8: # Exit codes:
9: #   0 = available (API responded normally)
10: #   1 = rate-limited (usage limit reached)
11: #   2 = auth invalid or expired
12: #   3 = no auth file or no OpenAI entry
13: #
14: # Usage:
15: #   usage-check.sh              # human-readable output
16: #   usage-check.sh --quiet      # exit code only
17: #   usage-check.sh --json       # raw API response
18: #   usage-check.sh --token TOKEN # use a specific token instead of auth.json
19: 
20: AUTH_FILE="${OPENCODE_AUTH_FILE:-$HOME/.local/share/opencode/auth.json}"
21: QUIET=false
22: JSON_MODE=false
23: TOKEN_OVERRIDE=""
24: 
25: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
26:     case "$1" in
27:     --quiet | -q)
28:         QUIET=true
29:         shift
30:         ;;
31:     --json | -j)
32:         JSON_MODE=true
33:         shift
34:         ;;
35:     --token)
36:         TOKEN_OVERRIDE="$2"
37:         shift 2
38:         ;;
39:     --auth-file)
40:         AUTH_FILE="$2"
41:         shift 2
42:         ;;
43:     *)
44:         shift
45:         ;;
46:     esac
47: done
48: 
49: log() {
50:     if ! $QUIET; then
51:         echo "$@"
52:     fi
53: }
54:

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: # Probe the OpenAI API to detect whether the current account is rate-limited.
5: # Reads the OAuth access token from OpenCode's auth.json and makes a minimal
6: # chat completion request (gpt-4o-mini, max_tokens=1).
7: #
8: # Exit codes:
9: #   0 = available (API responded normally)
10: #   1 = rate-limited (usage limit reached)
11: #   2 = auth invalid or expired
12: #   3 = no auth file or no OpenAI entry
13: #
14: # Usage:
15: #   usage-check.sh              # human-readable output
16: #   usage-check.sh --quiet      # exit code only
17: #   usage-check.sh --json       # raw API response
18: #   usage-check.sh --token TOKEN # use a specific token instead of auth.json
19: 
20: AUTH_FILE="${OPENCODE_AUTH_FILE:-$HOME/.local/share/opencode/auth.json}"
21: QUIET=false
22: JSON_MODE=false
23: TOKEN_OVERRIDE=""
24: 
25: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
26:     case "$1" in
27:     --quiet | -q)
28:         QUIET=true
29:         shift
30:         ;;
31:     --json | -j)
32:         JSON_MODE=true
33:         shift
34:         ;;
35:     --token)
36:         TOKEN_OVERRIDE="$2"
37:         shift 2
38:         ;;
39:     --auth-file)
40:         AUTH_FILE="$2"
41:         shift 2
42:         ;;
43:     *)
44:         shift
45:         ;;
46:     esac
47: done
48: 
49: log() {
50:     if ! $QUIET; then
51:         echo "$@"
52:     fi
53: }

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: set -euo pipefail
3: 
4: # Probe the OpenAI API to detect whether the current account is rate-limited.
5: # Reads the OAuth access token from OpenCode's auth.json and makes a minimal
6: # chat completion request (gpt-4o-mini, max_tokens=1).
7: #
8: # Exit codes:
9: #   0 = available (API responded normally)
10: #   1 = rate-limited (usage limit reached)
11: #   2 = auth invalid or expired
12: #   3 = no auth file or no OpenAI entry
13: #
14: # Usage:
15: #   usage-check.sh              # human-readable output
16: #   usage-check.sh --quiet      # exit code only
17: #   usage-check.sh --json       # raw API response
18: #   usage-check.sh --token TOKEN # use a specific token instead of auth.json
19: 
20: AUTH_FILE="${OPENCODE_AUTH_FILE:-$HOME/.local/share/opencode/auth.json}"
21: QUIET=false
22: JSON_MODE=false
23: TOKEN_OVERRIDE=""
24: 
25: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
26:     case "$1" in
27:     --quiet | -q)
28:         QUIET=true
29:         shift
30:         ;;
31:     --json | -j)
32:         JSON_MODE=true
33:         shift
34:         ;;
35:     --token)
36:         TOKEN_OVERRIDE="$2"
37:         shift 2
38:         ;;
39:     --auth-file)
40:         AUTH_FILE="$2"
41:         shift 2
42:         ;;
43:     *)
44:         shift
45:         ;;
46:     esac
47: done
48: 
49: log() {
50:     if ! $QUIET; then
51:         echo "$@"
52:     fi
53: }

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+DOTFILES_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
+CLAUDE_USAGE_SCRIPT="$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/ticket-queue/claude-usage.sh"
+OPENCODE_USAGE_SCRIPT="$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/opencode/usage-check.sh"
+
+INTERVAL=30
+ONCE=false
+NO_COLOR=false
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --once)
+            ONCE=true
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --interval)
+            INTERVAL="${2:?Error: --interval requires seconds}"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --no-color)
+            NO_COLOR=true
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --help|-h)
+            cat <<'EOF'
+subscription-dashboard.sh - Claude + OpenAI subscription usage dashboard
+
+Usage:
+  subscription-dashboard.sh            # Interactive auto-refresh view
+  subscription-dashboard.sh --once     # Print once and exit
+  subscription-dashboard.sh --interval 15
+  subscription-dashboard.sh --no-color
+EOF
+            exit 0
+            ;;
+        *)
+            echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2
+            exit 1
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+if [[ ! "$INTERVAL" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [[ "$INTERVAL" -lt 1 ]]; then
+    echo "Error: --interval must be a positive integer" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+if [[ -t 1 && "$NO_COLOR" == false ]]; then
+    C_RESET=$'\033[0m'
+    C_BOLD=$'\033[1m'
+    C_DIM=$'\033[2m'
+    C_RED=$'\033[31m'
+    C_GREEN=$'\033[32m'

parts.text:
**Analyzing tmux status bar**

I’m figuring out the answer plan mode without edits. I might need to mention the tmux status bar isn't using opencode. The number is possibly from the git branch in the current pane. Right now, there might be conflicts with the main branch. I'm considering active pane as timestamp. The number looks like a Unix epoch in milliseconds. It might relate to a gwt worktree or come from a command in the pane. I want to inspect the continuum_save output, but it’s read-only. Looking deeper into this!

**Troubleshooting timestamp output**

It seems like there’s a plugin bug that may cause it to output nothing or emit a timestamp. 1780341205 looks like a Unix timestamp; the current date is June 1, 2026. It also seems plausible that this value is the tmux-continuum autosave timestamp, shown twice in the screenshot. This occurs because of accidental printing from a save hook. I need to consider removing that command to stop it from displaying or possibly inspecting the continuum docs for more details on fixing this.

content.summary:
(b1)

User asked to "Make these changes" after the prior OpenCode startup diagnosis. Changes implemented in `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles`:

1. First startup optimization in `.config/opencode/plugin/claude-compat.ts`:
- Added `STARTUP_FAST_TIMEOUT_MS = 500` and `STARTUP_CONTEXT_TIMEOUT_MS = 2500`.
- Extended `runScript(...)` and `runCommand(...)` signatures with `timeoutMs = 0` and routed process completion through a new `waitForProcess(proc, timeoutMs)` helper. Helper races `proc.exited` with a timeout, calls `proc.kill()` on timeout, returns exit code `124`, and clears the timer.
- Added `startupContextMode()` reading `OPENCODE_STARTUP_CONTEXT`; valid modes are `off`, `full`, default `light`.
- On `session.created`, still resets session state, but now runs only `tmux-agent-start.sh` synchronously with the 500ms timeout, then `void collectSessionStartContext().catch(() => {})` fire-and-forget instead of awaiting all SessionStart work.
- `collectSessionStartContext()` returns immediately if `OPENCODE_STARTUP_CONTEXT=off`; default `light` runs only startup hygiene and context hooks; `full` opt-in runs dream count and `bd prime` unless disabled.
- Env flags added: `OPENCODE_STARTUP_CONTEXT=off|light|full`, `OPENCODE_DREAM_DISABLE=1`, `OPENCODE_BD_PRIME_DISABLE=1`, `OPENCODE_PLAN_RESUME_DISABLE=1`, `OPENCODE_CHANGELOG_RESUME_DISABLE=1`.
- `bd prime` and dream count moved to `mode === "full"`; heavy context scripts have 2500ms cap.

2. Harness update for async startup:
- `scripts/opencode/test-claude-compat.sh` generated JS harness gained `sleep(ms)` and changed the session-start changelog assertion from immediate check to polling up to 20 attempts with 50ms sleep, because SessionStart context now arrives asynchronously.

3. Validation after first optimization:
- `bun --check .config/opencode/plugin/claude-compat.ts` passed.
- `scripts/opencode/test-claude-compat.sh` passed.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` initially failed `OpenCode Claude compat harness passes` before harness adjustment, then passed `185/185` after the harness polling update.
- Final response told user OpenCode needs restart to load plugin changes.

User then said it was much faster and asked for further optimizations. Response recommended:
- Disable/scope OpenCode LSP for Terraform-heavy repos if slowness returns, but keep it for now and add cache ignores first.
- Add watcher ignores for infra/vendor caches.
- Parallelize deferred context scripts (`work-detect.sh`, `lsp-status.sh`, `plan-resume.sh`, `changelog-resume.sh`).
- TTL-gate maintenance scripts (`fix-hookify-imports.sh`, `plugin-chmod-fix.sh`) instead of running every session.
- Consider making `opencode-with-claude` opt-in/conditional.
- Add lightweight startup timing logs.
User asked if always-on LSPs are a net positive; answer explained not always: useful when bounded, net negative in large Terraform/IaC repos due `terraform-ls` crawling modules/providers/caches; recommendation was keep LSP enabled for now, add aggressive Terraform/cache ignores, and only scope/disable if slowness returns.

User then asked to implement points 3 and 4: "Parallelize deferred context scripts... Stop running maintenance scripts every session... These two points seem appropriate to implement". Implemented in `.config/opencode/plugin/claude-compat.ts`:
- Added imports `createHash` from `node:crypto`, and `mkdirSync`, `statSync`; later switched `join(stampPath, "..")` to `dirname(stampPath)` and imported `dirname` from `node:path`.
- Added `MAINTENANCE_TTL_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000`.
- Added `addContextResult(result: HookResult)` to centralize stdout/stderr session context injection.
- Added `maintenanceStampPath(name)` returning `~/.cache/opencode/claude-compat-maintenance/<sha256(projectDir).slice(0,16)>/<name>.stamp`.
- Added `shouldRunMaintenance(name)`; skips if `OPENCODE_MAINTENANCE_DISABLE=1`, skips if stamp mtime newer than TTL, otherwise creates parent dir and writes ISO timestamp best-effort before returning true.
- Added `runMaintenanceScript(name, script)` that skips or runs the maintenance hook with startup context timeout.
- Replaced startup tasks for `fix-hookify-imports.sh` and `plugin-chmod-fix.sh` with `runMaintenanceScript("fix-hookify-imports", ...)` and `runMaintenanceScript("plugin-chmod-fix", ...)`.
- Replaced sequential `await runContextScript(...)` calls for `work-detect.sh`, `lsp-status.sh`, optional `plan-resume.sh`, optional `changelog-resume.sh` with a `contextScripts` array, `Promise.all(contextScripts.map(...runScript...))`, then a `for` loop over results calling `addContextResult(result)` to preserve stable injection order despite parallel execution.
- Removed now-unused `runContextScript` after grep found only its definition remained.
- Final validation passed: `bun --check .config/opencode/plugin/claude-compat.ts`, `scripts/opencode/test-claude-compat.sh`, and `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` `185/185`.

User later committed `speed up opencode` themselves (`83d55325`) after `git add .`, but `.opencode/plugins/` still appeared untracked. Investigation showed `.opencode/plugins` was an empty/generated directory in this view; `.gitignore` had no `.opencode/plugins/` ignore. User said they still saw it, so `.gitignore` was edited under "Generated agent runtime surfaces" to add:
`.opencode/plugins/`
Verified with `git check-ignore -v .opencode/plugins/`, output `.gitignore:65:.opencode/plugins/ .opencode/plugins/`. Final response explained it is generated OpenCode/plugin runtime/cache state and now ignored.

User asked: "Is it worth enabling doom_loop to true in our opencode config? https://opencode.ai/docs/permissions/" Investigation fetched OpenCode permissions docs and config schema. Important findings:
- `doom_loop` is a permission key triggered when the same tool call repeats 3 times with identical input.
- Schema says `doom_loop` is a `PermissionActionConfig`, not boolean; valid values are `"ask"`, `"allow"`, `"deny"`.
- Current `.config/opencode/opencode.json` had line 68 `"permission": "allow"`, meaning everything likely allowed including doom-loop guard.
Recommendation: use object form preserving allow default but making doom-loop ask:
```json
"permission": {
  "*": "allow",
  "doom_loop": "ask"
}
```
User then said "Implement this". Changes made:
- `.config/opencode/opencode.json`: replaced `"permission": "allow"` with object above.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh`: existing test at line ~1062 was `OpenCode permissions are blanket allow` checking `jq -r '.permission' == 'allow'`; updated to `OpenCode permissions allow by default` checking `jq -e '.permission["*"] == "allow" and .permission.doom_loop == "ask"'`.
Validation:
- `jq empty .config/opencode/opencode.json` passed.
- `scripts/test-filter.sh opencode` initially failed the old blanket-allow test, then after test update passed `185/185`.
- Final status at that point showed modified `.config/opencode/opencode.json`, `.tmux/resurrect/last` (not changed by assistant), and `scripts/test-filter.sh`.

Important constraints/context:
- User and assistant are in `/Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles`.
- Do not revert user changes. `.tmux/resurrect/last` is user/runtime modified and should not be touched unless asked.
- OpenCode config/plugin changes require OpenCode restart to take effect.
- The latest active user question after this compressed range concerns an image of a flickering number/status line in OpenCode/tmux; answer/investigation should continue from visible latest messages.

The plan mode prevents edits, but I can explain the situation. I’ll need to mention that the number 1780341205 likely comes from the tmux-continuum script output leaking into the status-right. So, I’ll inspect the script and gather more insights. Let's see what I can find!

<path>/Users/shahe.islam/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: 
3: CURRENT_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"
4: 
5: source "$CURRENT_DIR/helpers.sh"
6: source "$CURRENT_DIR/variables.sh"
7: source "$CURRENT_DIR/shared.sh"
8: 
9: supported_tmux_version_ok() {
10: 	"$CURRENT_DIR/check_tmux_version.sh" "$SUPPORTED_VERSION"
11: }
12: 
13: get_interval() {
14: 	get_tmux_option "$auto_save_interval_option" "$auto_save_interval_default"
15: }
16: 
17: auto_save_not_disabled() {
18: 	[ "$(get_interval)" -gt 0 ]
19: }
20: 
21: enough_time_since_last_run_passed() {
22: 	local last_saved_timestamp="$(get_tmux_option "$last_auto_save_option" "0")"
23: 	local interval_minutes="$(get_interval)"
24: 	local interval_seconds="$((interval_minutes * 60))"
25: 	local next_run="$((last_saved_timestamp + $interval_seconds))"
26: 	[ "$(current_timestamp)" -ge "$next_run" ]
27: }
28: 
29: fetch_and_run_tmux_resurrect_save_script() {
30: 	local resurrect_save_script_path="$(get_tmux_option "$resurrect_save_path_option" "")"
31: 	if [ -n "$resurrect_save_script_path" ]; then
32: 		"$resurrect_save_script_path" "quiet" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
33: 		set_last_save_timestamp
34: 	fi
35: }
36: 
37: acquire_lock() {
38: 	# Sometimes tmux starts multiple saves in parallel. We want only one
39: 	# save to be running, otherwise we can get corrupted saved state.
40: 	local lockdir_prefix="/tmp/tmux-continuum-$(current_tmux_server_pid)-lock-"
41: 	# The following implements a lock that auto-expires after 100...200s.
42: 	local lock_generation=$((`date +%s` / 100))
43: 	local lockdir1="${lockdir_prefix}${lock_generation}"
44: 	local lockdir2="${lockdir_prefix}$(($lock_generation + 1))"
45: 	if mkdir "$lockdir1"; then
46: 		trap "rmdir "$lockdir1"" EXIT
47: 		if mkdir "$lockdir2"; then
48: 			trap "rmdir "$lockdir1" "$lockdir2"" EXIT
49: 			return 0
50: 		fi
51: 	fi
52: 	return 1  # Someone else has the lock.
53: }
54: 
55: main() {
56: 	if supported_tmux_version_ok && auto_save_not_disabled && enough_time_since_last_run_passed && acquire_lock; then
57: 		fetch_and_run_tmux_resurrect_save_script
58: 	fi
59: }
60: main

(End of file - total 60 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
That number is not OpenCode usage. It is a Unix timestamp leaking from `tmux-continuum`.

Evidence:
- Your live tmux `status-right` starts with:
  `#(/Users/shahe.islam/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh)...`
- `tmux-continuum` stores the last autosave time in:
  `@continuum-save-last-timestamp`
- Current value is the same kind of number: `1780341149`
- The number in the screenshot, `1780341205`, is seconds since epoch, i.e. an autosave timestamp.

Why it flickers:
- tmux re-renders the status bar on `status-interval` currently `15`.
- `tmux-continuum` hooks into `status-right` by running `continuum_save.sh`.
- That command’s stdout is being displayed, so the timestamp appears and redraws/flickers.
- The duplicate `17803412051780341205` suggests the continuum status hook/output is being rendered twice or concatenated during refresh.

Fix plan:
1. Keep `tmux-continuum` autosave enabled.
2. Override `status-right` after TPM/plugin loading.
3. Keep the continuum command in `#(...)`, but redirect its output: `>/dev/null 2>&1`.
4. Preserve your branch/time status display.

Conceptually, the final status segment should use:

```tmux
#($HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh >/dev/null 2>&1)
```

instead of the current visible-output form.

Since you’re in plan mode, I have not edited anything.
Why isn't this flcikering happening on other devices isnt it all centrally configured from ~/dotfiles

**Inspecting plugin configuration**

I need to figure this out. Maybe I should check the current plugin order in the config. It's important to clarify the difference between what's intended in the .tmux.conf file and what happens at runtime. The TPM plugin seems to modify the status-right at runtime. Oddly, despite the line 257 in the .tmux.conf not showing it, the live status-right still includes continuum prepended. I’ll need to inspect the tmux configuration around TPM more closely.
I’ll verify where the repo config differs from the live tmux server state, because this looks like runtime/plugin state rather than the canonical `~/.tmux.conf` line itself.

state.title: .tmux.conf

parts.text:
Because `~/dotfiles` centralizes the source config, but this flicker is coming from live tmux/plugin runtime state, which is not fully centralized.

What I found:

- Repo config line `.tmux.conf:257` does not include the timestamp.
- Live tmux server has this extra prefix in `status-right`:
  `#(/Users/shahe.islam/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh)...`
- That prefix is added by `tmux-continuum` at runtime because `.tmux.conf:221-224` enables it.
- The visible number is `@continuum-save-last-timestamp`, i.e. the last autosave epoch timestamp.

Why only this device likely flickers:

- This tmux server may have been running across config changes; tmux options are live state until the server is restarted or config is re-sourced cleanly.
- TPM plugins live under `~/.tmux/plugins/`, not in `~/dotfiles`, so plugin versions can differ per device.
- `tmux-continuum` mutates `status-right` dynamically; that mutation can differ based on plugin version/load order.
- Other devices may not have `tmux-continuum` installed, may have a different version, or may have restarted tmux after newer config.
- Your `.tmux.conf` sets `status-right` before TPM loads, then TPM/continuum prepends its autosave hook, and there is no later `status-right` reassertion after TPM.

The durable fix is still in central config: add a final `status-right` override after TPM loads that runs continuum silently or removes its visible hook. Then every device will converge after `stow`/config reload/restart.

No edits made because plan mode is active.

diffs.patch:
Index: .tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- .tmux.conf	
+++ .tmux.conf	
@@ -1,382 +1,387 @@
 # Basic tmux configuration
 set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
 set -ga terminal-overrides ",*:Tc:RGB,xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7,tmux-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
 
 # Set prefix to Ctrl-s (more ergonomic than Ctrl-Space)
 set -g prefix C-s
 unbind C-b
 unbind C-a
 # Unbind C-z to ensure it's passed through to nested applications (like Neovim terminal)
 unbind C-z
 # Double tap Ctrl-s to send literal Ctrl-s to applications
 bind-key C-s send-prefix
 
 # Enable key repeat
 set -g repeat-time 0
 
 # Basic settings
 set -g mouse on
 set -g extended-keys on
 set -g extended-keys-format csi-u
 set -g escape-time 0
 set -g focus-events on
 set -g history-limit 100000
 set -g base-index 1
 setw -g pane-base-index 1
 set -g renumber-windows on
 setw -g automatic-rename off
 
 # Prevent Claude Code nested-session false positives in tmux
 set-environment -gr CLAUDECODE
 
 # When a session is destroyed, switch to the most recently active session
 # instead of detaching the client (which would close the terminal).
 # Tested with tmux 3.5a.
 set -g detach-on-destroy off
 
 # Proactively ensure "main" session exists at server start.
 # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback
 # target is always available for detach-on-destroy.
 # exit-empty is set to off so the server survives the brief window between
 # sole-session destruction and hook recreation.
 set -g exit-empty off
 run-shell 'tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null || tmux new-session -d -s main'
 
 # Reactively recreate "main" if destroyed while the server is still running.
 set-hook -g session-closed 'if-shell "! tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null" "new-session -d -s main"'
 
 # Activity monitoring - custom completion detection
 # Monitors activity, then checks for completion after silence period
 setw -g monitor-activity on
 set -g visual-activity off  # Don't show visual alerts
 set -g activity-action any  # Trigger on ALL windows including current (enables Claude highlighting)
 # Disable visual styling for windows with activity (no grey background)
 set -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#97958e,bg=#262029"
 
 # Disable silence monitoring (alert-silence hook doesn't fire reliably)
 setw -g monitor-silence 0
 set -g visual-silence off
 
 # Agent activity monitoring:
 #   Claude: event-driven via scripts/bin/claude wrapper (injects --settings hooks)
 #   OpenCode: event-driven via tmux-status plugin plus tmux-open state polling
 # Explicitly unset stale hooks from earlier config versions
 set-hook -gu alert-activity
 set-hook -gu after-select-window
 
 # Bell on activity completion - disabled
 set -g bell-action none
 set -g visual-bell off  # Disable visual bell flash
 
 # Clipboard integration
 set -g set-clipboard on
 # Allow OSC-52 escape sequences to pass through to the terminal
 # Required for Neovim clipboard to work in SSH/container sessions
 set -g allow-passthrough on
 
 # OS-specific default shell configuration
 # reattach-to-user-namespace removed: not needed since macOS 12 (Monterey)
 # pbcopy/pbpaste and OSC-52 work natively in tmux on modern macOS
 # NOTE: default-command is set here to block tmux-sensible from overriding it
 # with "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL" (which spawns zsh, not fish).
 # After TPM loads, default-command is reset to empty (see post-TPM section)
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly without nesting.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g default-shell /opt/homebrew/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /opt/homebrew/bin/fish" \
   "set -g default-shell /usr/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /usr/bin/fish"
 
 # Enhanced Copy Mode Configuration
 # Use vi-style keys in copy mode
 setw -g mode-keys vi
 
 # Easy copy mode entry with prefix
 # Use v with prefix (Ctrl-s v) instead of [
 bind-key v copy-mode
 # Keep the default [ binding as well
 bind-key [ copy-mode
 
 # Copy mode key bindings (vi-style)
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
 # Copy to the system clipboard and exit copy mode, matching Vim yank expectations.
 # OS-specific clipboard command: pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Clean copy: Y joins lines (fixes copy-paste line breaks from TUI apps like Claude Code)
 # Regular y = raw copy, Y = join lines (for commands), Alt-y = smart (preserve paragraphs)
 # Note: C-y is NOT used here as it conflicts with vi copy-mode scroll-up
 # Note: M-y requires terminal Alt/Meta key support (works in Ghostty/WezTerm/iTerm2).
 #   Y (Shift-y) is the primary binding and works in all terminals.
 # Note: tmux smart mode is a lightweight paragraph-joiner. For full-featured mode
 #   with list/indent/code-fence preservation, use Fish: cfx -p
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'"
 
 # Additional convenient bindings for copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel
 # Match Neovim scroll convention: C-d scrolls up, C-f scrolls down.
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-f send-keys -X halfpage-down
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-y send-keys -X scroll-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-e send-keys -X scroll-down
 # Double-click to select word, triple-click to select line
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi DoubleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-word
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi TripleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-line
 
 # Search bindings in copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -i -p "search down" "send -X search-forward-incremental \"%%%\""
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -i -p "search up" "send -X search-backward-incremental \"%%%\""
 
 # Key bindings
 # NOTE: Context-aware 'r' key binding is defined AFTER TPM loads (see bottom of file)
 # to override plugin reload bindings
 
 # Window management
 bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 # Pane navigation handled by vim-tmux-navigator (Ctrl-h/j/k/l)
 # Keeping prefix+h/j/k/l as fallback when vim-tmux-navigator is not active
 # bind h select-pane -L
 # bind j select-pane -D
 # bind k select-pane -U
 # bind l select-pane -R
 
 # Smart adaptive splitting - automatically chooses horizontal or vertical based on pane dimensions
 # Uses native tmux format arithmetic (no shell spawn): 8*width vs 20*height accounts for ~2:1 char aspect ratio
 bind -r Space if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical#[default]'"
 
 # Respawn pane keybinding (restart dead/frozen process)
 bind R respawn-pane -k \; display '#[fg=#d3daad] Pane Respawned#[default]'
 
 # M and C-m bindings removed — watcher colors are purely realtime, no manual clear needed
 
 # Toggle logging - save pane output to a file (press again to stop)
 bind L pipe-pane -o "cat >> ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log" \; \
   display '#[fg=#efcfab]📝 Logging: ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log#[default]'
 
 # List of plugins
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
 # set -g @plugin 'alexwforsythe/tmux-which-key'  # Disabled - conflicts with Space key binding
 set -g @plugin 'sainnhe/tmux-fzf'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-open'
 # Configure tmux-open
 set -g @open-S 'https://www.google.com/search?q='
 # Use 'o' to open URLs/files in copy mode
 set -g @open 'o'
 # Use Ctrl-o to open in editor
 set -g @open-editor 'C-o'
 # tmux-copycat removed: deprecated, slow regex on scrollback. Use native / search in copy mode
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control'
 # vim-tmux-navigator: seamless navigation between vim and tmux
 set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
 # tmux-thumbs - fast Rust-based text hints (replacement for tmux-fingers)
 set -g @plugin 'fcsonline/tmux-thumbs'
 set -g @thumbs-key F
 # OS-specific clipboard command for tmux-thumbs
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | pbcopy'" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | xclip -selection clipboard'"
 set -g @thumbs-upcase-command 'open {}'  # Shift+hint opens URL/path
 set -g @thumbs-hint-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-hint-bg-color black
 set -g @thumbs-select-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-select-bg-color black
 
 # tmux-fzf configuration - unified FZF menu for tmux operations
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + f (Find/FZF menu)
 TMUX_FZF_LAUNCH_KEY="f"
 TMUX_FZF_OPTIONS="-p -w 80% -h 80% -m"
 TMUX_FZF_ORDER="session|window|pane|command|keybinding|clipboard"
 
 # tmux session persistence across server/device restarts
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
 set -g @resurrect-processes 'nvim vim "~ocv->ocv"'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
 # Claude Code is intentionally excluded here.
 # OpenCode is restored through the local ocv wrapper.
 
 # CPU/battery status plugins removed - loaded but never displayed in native status.
 # tmux-smooth-scroll removed: every scroll tick spawns bash→bash→perl chain
 # Native tmux scrolling is instant and sufficient
 
 # Keep tmux-continuum last: it hooks into status-right for autosave.
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
 set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
 set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
 
 # Miniautumn theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
 
 # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
 bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
 
 # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
 bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
 
 # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
 # set -g @which-key-popup 1
 
 # =============================================================================
 # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Native Three Section Layout
 # =============================================================================
 # Session (left) | Windows (center) | Git + datetime (right)
 # Window names read @wname_style so agent active/idle colors render directly.
 # =============================================================================
 
 # Status bar layout configuration
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
 set -g status-left-length 30              # Space for session indicator
 set -g status-right-length 80             # Space for Git branch and time
 set -g status-interval 15                 # Git branch + datetime refresh cadence
 
 # Calm transparent status bar styling. Segment backgrounds use default so
 # terminal opacity/blur can show through; the current tab keeps a soft highlight.
 set -g status-style "bg=default,fg=#d7d5cd"
 set -g status-left "#[fg=#97958e,bg=default] #S #[default]"
 set -g status-right '#[fg=#a7e1e8,bg=default]#(branch=$(git -C "#{pane_current_path}" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ]; then dirty=""; git -C "#{pane_current_path}" diff --quiet --ignore-submodules -- 2>/dev/null || dirty="*"; printf " %s%s #[fg=#5e5762]·" "$branch" "$dirty"; fi)#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
 
 # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
 # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Window status styling. @wname_style is window-scoped and set by agent launchers.
 setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#f3f1e9,bg=#332d36,bold"
 setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6f6a73,bg=default"
 setw -g window-status-format '#[range=window|#{window_id}] #{window_index} #[fg=#5e5762]· #{?#{@wname_style},#{@wname_style},}#W '
 setw -g window-status-current-format '#[range=window|#{window_id}] #{window_index} #[fg=#5e5762]· #{?#{@wname_style},#{@wname_style},}#W#{?pane_synchronized, [sync],} '
 
 # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
 set -g pane-border-style "fg=#423b45"
 set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#e4caf1"
 
 # Message style
 set -g message-style "bg=#1a141d,fg=#d7d5cd"
 set -g message-command-style "bg=#1a141d,fg=#d7d5cd"
 
 # Copy mode colors
 set -g mode-style "bg=#423b45,fg=#f3f1e9"
 
 # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
 # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
 # set -g status-interval 30
 
 # Auto-install TPM if not present
 if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
 
 # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
 # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
 # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
 
 # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
 # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
 bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
 bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
 bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
 # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
 # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
 bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
 bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
 
 # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
 bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
 
 # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
 # Prefix + O: select previous pane
 # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
 
 # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
 run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
 
 # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
 run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
 
 # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane after plugin bindings load.
 # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
 # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
 bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
 
 # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
 bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
 
 # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre
 
 # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 set -g popup-border-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Keep native status refresh interval after TPM plugins load.
 set -g status-interval 15
 
+# tmux-continuum prepends its autosave hook to status-right at plugin load time.
+# Reassert the native right status here and run continuum silently so its
+# last-save epoch timestamp never leaks into the visible status bar.
+set -g status-right '#(~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh >/dev/null 2>&1)#[fg=#a7e1e8,bg=default]#(branch=$(git -C "#{pane_current_path}" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ]; then dirty=""; git -C "#{pane_current_path}" diff --quiet --ignore-submodules -- 2>/dev/null || dirty="*"; printf " %s%s #[fg=#5e5762]·" "$branch" "$dirty"; fi)#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
+
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
 set -g default-command ''
 
 # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
 # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
 # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
 # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
 # Agent colors use @wname_style: working=red, idle=yellow, exited=default.
 set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
 bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#d3daad' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#a7e1e8]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#97958e](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#97958e]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#efcfab](attached),}}}'
 bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#d3daad' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#a7e1e8]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#97958e](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#97958e]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#efcfab](attached),}}}'
 
 # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
 # Must be after TPM to override plugin reload bindings
 bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#d3daad]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

parts.text: Make the changes

**Updating tmux configuration**

I need to edit the tmux configuration, and I'm considering if I should use a todo list to keep track of this. Inspecting the current status before making edits seems wise. I’m thinking about adding a patch after running tpm and keeping the continuum plugin silent since it modifies the status immediately. Adding a line to set status-right and updating the comments will be crucial. I want to make sure the tmux group tests are applied as well. Let's apply this patch!
I’ll add a final post-TPM `status-right` override so `tmux-continuum` still autosaves but its timestamp output is redirected away, then run the tmux-focused validation.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
@@
 # Keep native status refresh interval after TPM plugins load.
 set -g status-interval 15
+
+# tmux-continuum prepends its autosave hook to status-right at plugin load time.
+# Reassert the native right status here and run continuum silently so its
+# last-save epoch timestamp never leaks into the visible status bar.
+set -g status-right '#(~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh >/dev/null 2>&1)#[fg=#a7e1e8,bg=default]#(branch=$(git -C "#{pane_current_path}" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ]; then dirty=""; git -C "#{pane_current_path}" diff --quiet --ignore-submodules -- 2>/dev/null || dirty="*"; printf " %s%s #[fg=#5e5762]·" "$branch" "$dirty"; fi)#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
 
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
*** End Patch

files.patch:
Index: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
+++ /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf
@@ -349,8 +349,13 @@
 
 # Keep native status refresh interval after TPM plugins load.
 set -g status-interval 15
 
+# tmux-continuum prepends its autosave hook to status-right at plugin load time.
+# Reassert the native right status here and run continuum silently so its
+# last-save epoch timestamp never leaks into the visible status bar.
+set -g status-right '#(~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh >/dev/null 2>&1)#[fg=#a7e1e8,bg=default]#(branch=$(git -C "#{pane_current_path}" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ]; then dirty=""; git -C "#{pane_current_path}" diff --quiet --ignore-submodules -- 2>/dev/null || dirty="*"; printf " %s%s #[fg=#5e5762]·" "$branch" "$dirty"; fi)#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
+
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty

parts.text:
Implemented the tmux fix.

Changed:
- `.tmux.conf` now reasserts `status-right` after TPM/plugins load.
- It still runs `tmux-continuum` autosave, but redirects its output with `>/dev/null 2>&1`, so the epoch timestamp cannot leak into the visible status bar.

Applied to current tmux server:
- Ran `tmux source-file /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.tmux.conf`
- Live `status-right` now starts with the silent continuum hook.

Validation:
- `scripts/test-filter.sh tmux` passed: `45/45`.

Only intended file modified:
- `.tmux.conf`

diffs.patch:
Index: .tmux.conf
===================================================================
--- .tmux.conf	
+++ .tmux.conf	
@@ -1,387 +1,389 @@
 # Basic tmux configuration
 set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
 set -ga terminal-overrides ",*:Tc:RGB,xterm-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7,tmux-256color:Ms=\\E]52;c;%p2%s\\7"
 
 # Set prefix to Ctrl-s (more ergonomic than Ctrl-Space)
 set -g prefix C-s
 unbind C-b
 unbind C-a
 # Unbind C-z to ensure it's passed through to nested applications (like Neovim terminal)
 unbind C-z
 # Double tap Ctrl-s to send literal Ctrl-s to applications
 bind-key C-s send-prefix
 
 # Enable key repeat
 set -g repeat-time 0
 
 # Basic settings
 set -g mouse on
 set -g extended-keys on
 set -g extended-keys-format csi-u
 set -g escape-time 0
 set -g focus-events on
 set -g history-limit 100000
 set -g base-index 1
 setw -g pane-base-index 1
 set -g renumber-windows on
 setw -g automatic-rename off
 
 # Prevent Claude Code nested-session false positives in tmux
 set-environment -gr CLAUDECODE
 
 # When a session is destroyed, switch to the most recently active session
 # instead of detaching the client (which would close the terminal).
 # Tested with tmux 3.5a.
 set -g detach-on-destroy off
 
 # Proactively ensure "main" session exists at server start.
 # Combined with the session-closed hook below, this guarantees a fallback
 # target is always available for detach-on-destroy.
 # exit-empty is set to off so the server survives the brief window between
 # sole-session destruction and hook recreation.
 set -g exit-empty off
 run-shell 'tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null || tmux new-session -d -s main'
 
 # Reactively recreate "main" if destroyed while the server is still running.
 set-hook -g session-closed 'if-shell "! tmux has-session -t main 2>/dev/null" "new-session -d -s main"'
 
 # Activity monitoring - custom completion detection
 # Monitors activity, then checks for completion after silence period
 setw -g monitor-activity on
 set -g visual-activity off  # Don't show visual alerts
 set -g activity-action any  # Trigger on ALL windows including current (enables Claude highlighting)
 # Disable visual styling for windows with activity (no grey background)
 set -g window-status-activity-style "fg=#97958e,bg=#262029"
 
 # Disable silence monitoring (alert-silence hook doesn't fire reliably)
 setw -g monitor-silence 0
 set -g visual-silence off
 
 # Agent activity monitoring:
 #   Claude: event-driven via scripts/bin/claude wrapper (injects --settings hooks)
 #   OpenCode: event-driven via tmux-status plugin plus tmux-open state polling
 # Explicitly unset stale hooks from earlier config versions
 set-hook -gu alert-activity
 set-hook -gu after-select-window
 
 # Bell on activity completion - disabled
 set -g bell-action none
 set -g visual-bell off  # Disable visual bell flash
 
 # Clipboard integration
 set -g set-clipboard on
 # Allow OSC-52 escape sequences to pass through to the terminal
 # Required for Neovim clipboard to work in SSH/container sessions
 set -g allow-passthrough on
 
 # OS-specific default shell configuration
 # reattach-to-user-namespace removed: not needed since macOS 12 (Monterey)
 # pbcopy/pbpaste and OSC-52 work natively in tmux on modern macOS
 # NOTE: default-command is set here to block tmux-sensible from overriding it
 # with "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL" (which spawns zsh, not fish).
 # After TPM loads, default-command is reset to empty (see post-TPM section)
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly without nesting.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g default-shell /opt/homebrew/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /opt/homebrew/bin/fish" \
   "set -g default-shell /usr/bin/fish; \
    set -g default-command /usr/bin/fish"
 
 # Enhanced Copy Mode Configuration
 # Use vi-style keys in copy mode
 setw -g mode-keys vi
 
 # Easy copy mode entry with prefix
 # Use v with prefix (Ctrl-s v) instead of [
 bind-key v copy-mode
 # Keep the default [ binding as well
 bind-key [ copy-mode
 
 # Copy mode key bindings (vi-style)
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-v send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
 # Copy to the system clipboard and exit copy mode, matching Vim yank expectations.
 # OS-specific clipboard command: pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Clean copy: Y joins lines (fixes copy-paste line breaks from TUI apps like Claude Code)
 # Regular y = raw copy, Y = join lines (for commands), Alt-y = smart (preserve paragraphs)
 # Note: C-y is NOT used here as it conflicts with vi copy-mode scroll-up
 # Note: M-y requires terminal Alt/Meta key support (works in Ghostty/WezTerm/iTerm2).
 #   Y (Shift-y) is the primary binding and works in all terminals.
 # Note: tmux smart mode is a lightweight paragraph-joiner. For full-featured mode
 #   with list/indent/code-fence preservation, use Fish: cfx -p
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh join'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi M-y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-copy-cleanup.sh smart'"
 
 # Additional convenient bindings for copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X begin-selection
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel
 # Match Neovim scroll convention: C-d scrolls up, C-f scrolls down.
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-f send-keys -X halfpage-down
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-y send-keys -X scroll-up
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi C-e send-keys -X scroll-down
 # Double-click to select word, triple-click to select line
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi DoubleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-word
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi TripleClick1Pane select-pane \; send-keys -X select-line
 
 # Search bindings in copy mode
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi / command-prompt -i -p "search down" "send -X search-forward-incremental \"%%%\""
 bind-key -T copy-mode-vi ? command-prompt -i -p "search up" "send -X search-backward-incremental \"%%%\""
 
 # Key bindings
 # NOTE: Context-aware 'r' key binding is defined AFTER TPM loads (see bottom of file)
 # to override plugin reload bindings
 
 # Window management
 bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind % split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 bind '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
 # Pane navigation handled by vim-tmux-navigator (Ctrl-h/j/k/l)
 # Keeping prefix+h/j/k/l as fallback when vim-tmux-navigator is not active
 # bind h select-pane -L
 # bind j select-pane -D
 # bind k select-pane -U
 # bind l select-pane -R
 
 # Smart adaptive splitting - automatically chooses horizontal or vertical based on pane dimensions
 # Uses native tmux format arithmetic (no shell spawn): 8*width vs 20*height accounts for ~2:1 char aspect ratio
 bind -r Space if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical#[default]'"
 
 # Respawn pane keybinding (restart dead/frozen process)
 bind R respawn-pane -k \; display '#[fg=#d3daad] Pane Respawned#[default]'
 
 # M and C-m bindings removed — watcher colors are purely realtime, no manual clear needed
 
 # Toggle logging - save pane output to a file (press again to stop)
 bind L pipe-pane -o "cat >> ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log" \; \
   display '#[fg=#efcfab]📝 Logging: ~/dotfiles/tmux-logs/\$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)-#W.log#[default]'
 
 # List of plugins
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-yank'
 # set -g @plugin 'alexwforsythe/tmux-which-key'  # Disabled - conflicts with Space key binding
 set -g @plugin 'sainnhe/tmux-fzf'
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-open'
 # Configure tmux-open
 set -g @open-S 'https://www.google.com/search?q='
 # Use 'o' to open URLs/files in copy mode
 set -g @open 'o'
 # Use Ctrl-o to open in editor
 set -g @open-editor 'C-o'
 # tmux-copycat removed: deprecated, slow regex on scrollback. Use native / search in copy mode
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control'
 # vim-tmux-navigator: seamless navigation between vim and tmux
 set -g @plugin 'christoomey/vim-tmux-navigator'
 # tmux-thumbs - fast Rust-based text hints (replacement for tmux-fingers)
 set -g @plugin 'fcsonline/tmux-thumbs'
 set -g @thumbs-key F
 # OS-specific clipboard command for tmux-thumbs
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | pbcopy'" \
   "set -g @thumbs-command 'echo -n {} | xclip -selection clipboard'"
 set -g @thumbs-upcase-command 'open {}'  # Shift+hint opens URL/path
 set -g @thumbs-hint-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-hint-bg-color black
 set -g @thumbs-select-fg-color yellow
 set -g @thumbs-select-bg-color black
 
 # tmux-fzf configuration - unified FZF menu for tmux operations
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + f (Find/FZF menu)
 TMUX_FZF_LAUNCH_KEY="f"
 TMUX_FZF_OPTIONS="-p -w 80% -h 80% -m"
 TMUX_FZF_ORDER="session|window|pane|command|keybinding|clipboard"
 
 # tmux session persistence across server/device restarts
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
 set -g @resurrect-processes 'nvim vim "~ocv->ocv"'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
 set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
 # Claude Code is intentionally excluded here.
 # OpenCode is restored through the local ocv wrapper.
 
 # CPU/battery status plugins removed - loaded but never displayed in native status.
 # tmux-smooth-scroll removed: every scroll tick spawns bash→bash→perl chain
 # Native tmux scrolling is instant and sufficient
 
 # Keep tmux-continuum last: it hooks into status-right for autosave.
 set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
 set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
-set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
+# Disable continuum's status-right poller; dotfiles starts a quiet autosave loop
+# after TPM loads so the status bar never executes shell commands while drawing.
+set -g @continuum-save-interval '0'
+set -g @dotfiles-continuum-save-interval '15'
 
 # Miniautumn theme with omerxx-inspired aesthetics
 
 # Session Management - Single unified manager with tmuxinator integration
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + S
 bind S display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-manager.sh"
 
 # Session Trash Recovery - Browse and restore killed sessions
 # Access with: Ctrl-s + T
 bind T display-popup -E -h 80% -w 80% "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-session-trash.sh restore"
 
 # Which-key configuration (disabled - conflicts with Space binding)
 # set -g @which-key-popup 1
 
 # =============================================================================
 # STATUS BAR CONFIGURATION - Native Three Section Layout
 # =============================================================================
-# Session (left) | Windows (center) | Git + datetime (right)
+# Session (left) | Windows (center) | datetime (right)
 # Window names read @wname_style so agent active/idle colors render directly.
 # =============================================================================
 
 # Status bar layout configuration
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre              # Center window list
 set -g status-left-length 30              # Space for session indicator
-set -g status-right-length 80             # Space for Git branch and time
-set -g status-interval 15                 # Git branch + datetime refresh cadence
+set -g status-right-length 20             # Space for datetime
+set -g status-interval 15                 # datetime refresh cadence
 
 # Calm transparent status bar styling. Segment backgrounds use default so
 # terminal opacity/blur can show through; the current tab keeps a soft highlight.
 set -g status-style "bg=default,fg=#d7d5cd"
 set -g status-left "#[fg=#97958e,bg=default] #S #[default]"
-set -g status-right '#[fg=#a7e1e8,bg=default]#(branch=$(git -C "#{pane_current_path}" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ]; then dirty=""; git -C "#{pane_current_path}" diff --quiet --ignore-submodules -- 2>/dev/null || dirty="*"; printf " %s%s #[fg=#5e5762]·" "$branch" "$dirty"; fi)#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
+set -g status-right '#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
 
 # Ensure pane backgrounds are not forced to a solid color
 # (lets terminal/wezterm transparency show through and enables nvim winblend)
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Window status styling. @wname_style is window-scoped and set by agent launchers.
 setw -g window-status-current-style "fg=#f3f1e9,bg=#332d36,bold"
 setw -g window-status-style "fg=#6f6a73,bg=default"
 setw -g window-status-format '#[range=window|#{window_id}] #{window_index} #[fg=#5e5762]· #{?#{@wname_style},#{@wname_style},}#W '
 setw -g window-status-current-format '#[range=window|#{window_id}] #{window_index} #[fg=#5e5762]· #{?#{@wname_style},#{@wname_style},}#W#{?pane_synchronized, [sync],} '
 
 # Pane borders - subtle with accent on active
 set -g pane-border-style "fg=#423b45"
 set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=#e4caf1"
 
 # Message style
 set -g message-style "bg=#1a141d,fg=#d7d5cd"
 set -g message-command-style "bg=#1a141d,fg=#d7d5cd"
 
 # Copy mode colors
 set -g mode-style "bg=#423b45,fg=#f3f1e9"
 
 # # Update status bar every 30 seconds for CPU/RAM (OPTIMIZED: reduced from 10s)
 # # This reduces refresh overhead by 3x while still providing timely updates
 # set -g status-interval 30
 
 # Auto-install TPM if not present
 if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
    "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
 
 # Automatically split new windows and run claude (DISABLED)
 # This creates a vertical split with claude in the right pane for every new window
 # set-hook -g after-new-window 'split-window -h -p 40 "claude"'
 
 # Manual keybinding to open Claude Code when needed
 # Prefix + C opens Claude in a vertical split (40% width on the right)
 bind C split-window -h -p 40 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s a opens Claude with smart adaptive split
 bind a if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (Claude)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --effort max --remote-control' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (Claude)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s O opens OpenCode in its allow-permissions setup
 bind O if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (OpenCode)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/opencode/tmux-open.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (OpenCode)#[default]'"
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s e opens Neovim in current directory
 # C-c first to normalize Fish vi-mode state (lands us in insert mode at empty
 # prompt) so the typed `nvim .` isn't eaten as vi-mode normal commands.
 bind e send-keys C-c "nvim ." Enter
 
 # Quick launch: Ctrl-s E opens per-directory gwtt-prompt.local.md with smart adaptive split
 bind E if-shell -F "#{e|<:#{e|*:8,#{pane_width}},#{e|*:20,#{pane_height}}}" \
   "splitw -v -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#d3daad]↔ Split Horizontal (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'" \
   "splitw -h -c '#{pane_current_path}' '$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/gwtt-prompt-edit.sh' ; display '#[fg=#a7e1e8]↕ Split Vertical (gwtt-prompt)#[default]'"
 
 # URL handler binding (smart: tries visible pane first, falls back to full scrollback)
 bind-key o run-shell -b "$HOME/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-url-handler.sh"
 
 # Keep default tmux bindings for navigation
 # Prefix + O: select previous pane
 # Prefix + p: previous window (default tmux behavior)
 
 # Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
 run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
 
 # Force tmux-open bindings after all plugins load
 run-shell '~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-open/open.tmux'
 
 # Restore prefix+x to kill-pane after plugin bindings load.
 # Enhanced: capture window info before killing for worktree auto-cleanup.
 # Run the wrapper in tmux background so pane close stays responsive.
 bind-key x run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-kill-pane-cleanup.sh "#{session_name}" "#{window_name}" "#{window_panes}"'
 
 # Kill window: prefix + X (Shift-x) - closes entire window with worktree cleanup
 bind-key X confirm-before -p 'Kill window "#{window_name}"? (y/n)' \
   "run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-worktree-cleanup.sh \"#{session_name}\" \"#{window_name}\"'; kill-window"
 
 # Force status bar position and layout (override plugin defaults)
 set -g status-position bottom
 set -g status-justify centre
 
 # Final overrides to keep transparent panes/popups after plugins load
 set -g window-style 'bg=default'
 set -g window-active-style 'bg=default'
 set -g popup-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 set -g popup-border-style 'bg=default,fg=default'
 
 # Keep native status refresh interval after TPM plugins load.
 set -g status-interval 15
 
-# tmux-continuum prepends its autosave hook to status-right at plugin load time.
-# Reassert the native right status here and run continuum silently so its
-# last-save epoch timestamp never leaks into the visible status bar.
-set -g status-right '#(~/.tmux/plugins/tmux-continuum/scripts/continuum_save.sh >/dev/null 2>&1)#[fg=#a7e1e8,bg=default]#(branch=$(git -C "#{pane_current_path}" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$branch" ]; then dirty=""; git -C "#{pane_current_path}" diff --quiet --ignore-submodules -- 2>/dev/null || dirty="*"; printf " %s%s #[fg=#5e5762]·" "$branch" "$dirty"; fi)#[fg=#d7d5cd,bg=default] %H:%M '
+# Reassert the final status after TPM plugins load and start tmux persistence
+# from a quiet background loop instead of continuum's status-right command.
+run-shell -b '~/dotfiles/scripts/tmux/tmux-status-finalize.sh'
 
 # Fix nested fish: reset default-command to empty after TPM loads.
 # tmux-sensible (loaded by TPM) overrides empty default-command with
 # "reattach-to-user-namespace -l $SHELL". By setting it to fish BEFORE TPM,
 # sensible sees non-empty value and leaves it alone. Now we reset to empty
 # so tmux launches default-shell directly (no "fish -c fish" nesting).
 set -g default-command ''
 
 # Reassert clipboard copy-mode bindings after TPM plugins load.
 # tmux-yank can override these with legacy reattach-to-user-namespace commands.
 if-shell "uname | grep -q Darwin" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'" \
   "bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'; \
    bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'xclip -selection clipboard'"
 
 # Colored choose-tree session/window picker (must be after TPM)
 # Tree labels (session/window names, markers) use pane fg — set via select-pane -P
 # pane-mode-changed hook resets pane style when exiting choose-tree
 # Agent colors use @wname_style: working=red, idle=yellow, exited=default.
 set-hook -g pane-mode-changed[100] 'if -F "#{pane_in_mode}" "" "select-pane -P default"'
 bind s select-pane -P 'fg=#d3daad' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#a7e1e8]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#97958e](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#97958e]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#efcfab](attached),}}}'
 bind w select-pane -P 'fg=#d3daad' \; choose-tree -Zw -F '#{?pane_format,#[fg=#a7e1e8]#{pane_current_command} #[fg=#97958e](#{pane_current_path}),#{?window_format,#{@wname_style}#{window_name}#{?window_active, *,},#[fg=#97958e]#{session_windows} windows#{?session_attached, #[fg=#efcfab](attached),}}}'
 
 # Context-aware r key: recall when in Claude, config reload otherwise
 # Must be after TPM to override plugin reload bindings
 bind r if-shell "ps -o args= -t #{pane_tty} 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'claude'" \
     "confirm-before -p 'Kill Claude pane and run recall? (y/n)' 'respawn-pane -k \"/opt/homebrew/bin/recall\"'" \
     "source-file ~/.tmux.conf \\; display '#[fg=#d3daad]󰑓 Config Reloaded#[default]'"

Index: scripts/test-filter.sh
